narration: Create an input container div with the class name 'input-container'.
Inside the input container, add an input tag with type 'text', placeholder 'enter to do', and ID 'input-to-do'.
Add a button with ID 'add-to-do' and text content '+' inside the input container.
Create a to-do container div with the class name 'container' to hold the list items.
Inside the container, create a sample to-do div with class 'to-do' containing text 'write JavaScript code'.
Inside the to-do div, add a div with class 'actions' containing a checkbox input (type 'checkbox') and a delete button.
Copy the to-do div structure to create a second item with text 'write python code'.
In CSS, set the body font-family and background color to 'wheat'.
Style the H1 heading with text-align 'center'.
Apply display 'flex', flex-direction 'column', align-items 'center', and gap '10px' to the input-container.
Style the input element inside input-container with width '300px', height '30px', and font-size '1.25rem'.
Style the button inside input-container with width '40px', height '40px', border-radius '20px', font-size '1.5rem', and background color 'gold'.
Apply a 2px solid black border and 15px top margin to the container div.
Style the individual to-do div with display 'flex', align-items 'center', justify-content 'space-between', and a 2px solid red border.
Set the container to display 'flex', flex-direction 'column', gap '5px', width '400px', and align-items 'center'.
Style the to-do div with font-size '1.2rem', padding '0px 5px', border-radius '5px', and background color 'orange'. Remove the border.
Remove the border from the container div.

Style the delete button inside the actions div with width '75px', height '30px', background color 'red', color 'white', border 'dark gray', font-size '1.05rem', border-radius '10px', and margin-left '10px'.
In JavaScript, select the input element by ID 'input-to-do' and the add button by ID 'add-to-do'.
Select the to-do container using querySelector with the class '.container'.
Add a click event listener to the add-to-do button that calls a function named 'displayToDo'.
Inside the displayToDo function, create a new div element using document.createElement('div') and assign it the class 'to-do'.
Create a paragraph element for the to-do text, set its innerText to the value of input-to-do, and append it to the to-do div.
Create a div element for actions, assign it the class 'actions', and append it to the to-do div.
Create an input element for the checkbox, set its type attribute to 'checkbox', and append it to the actions div.
Create a button element for delete, set its innerText to 'delete', and append it to the actions div.
Append the newly created to-do div to the to-do container.
Clear the input field value after adding a new to-do item.
Add a conditional check to ensure the input value is not empty before creating a new to-do item.
Add a click event listener to the delete button that removes the parent to-do element from the container.
Remove or comment out the static HTML to-do items to allow dynamic generation only.
